name: Belleview Sports Complex
description: This location has three tennis courts with pickelball lines. Lights turn off at 10 pm.

name: Club Fore Ranch
description: Come play pickleball at Club Fore Ranch in Ocala, FL! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Clyatt Park
description: Clyatt Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 3 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. Players have access to lights, restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Coehadjoe Park
description: Coehadjoe Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 8 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Ernie Mills Park
description: Come play pickleball at Ernie Mills Park in Dunnellon, FL! There is 1 outdoor court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. The court is free. Reservations are not available.

name: Fort King Tennis Center
description: Come play pickleball at Fort King Tennis Center in Ocala, FL! There are 2 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms, water, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, and trainers or lessons. The courts are free.
open_play_schedule: No open play. Call Fort King Tennis Center to schedule lessons or reserve a court.

name: Frank DeLuca YMCA
description: Frank DeLuca YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 7 hard and wood courts. 3 are indoors and 4 are outdoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like lights and restrooms. A membership is required to play. You can reserve the courts.

name: Marion Oaks Community Center
description: Marion Oaks Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 4 outdoor hard and asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.

name: Mary Sue Rich Community Center
description: Laminate floors not hardwood. Play on Tuesday and Thursday from 9am-1pmTwo of the courts are four on four off, which are open to everyone . The far right court is a challenge court for higher level players . They also have lessons on Wednesday from 12pm-1pm.

name: Oak Run
description: Oak Run is a place to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 4 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are private. The facility includes amenities like lights.

name: Oak Run Pickleball Club
description: Members only

name: Ocala Palms
description: Ocala Palms is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 3 outdoor courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.

name: Ocala Preserve
description: Ocala Preserve is a place to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 6 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: On Top of the World - Recreation
description:
On Top of the World expanded from six (6) courts in 2012, to 29 courts today. Six of the courts are lighted, so residents can enjoy play from daylight to 9pm.

On Top of the World currently has a very popular pickleball resident club that plays regularly and holds an annual tournament every year. The club meets 7 days a week at 8:00 am, with each day being a different level play. Saturday mornings are reserved for teaching new players the sport. Sunday is open to all players. The club is actively working to expand play with other communities.
fees: Private — Guests and members play for free but are encouraged to join the league.

name: On Top Of The World - Weybourne
description: On Top Of The World - Weybourne is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 6 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to lights, restrooms, and water. The courts are private.
fees: Private — Access limited to residents of OTOW Weybourne Landings

name: Oxford Downs Poker Room
description: Oxford Downs Poker Room is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Summerfield, FL. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like food, restrooms, water, and lights. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play.

name: Pickleball Kingdom
description: Pickleball Kingdom is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 13 indoor concrete and acrylic courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include food, lights, locker rooms, a pro shop or equipment store, restrooms, trainers or lessons, water, wheelchair accessibility, youth programming, and adaptive programming. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Salt Springs Civic Association Park
description: Salt Springs Civic Association Park is a place to play pickleball in Fort McCoy, FL. There are 4 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Stone Creek Courts
description: Stone Creek Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ocala, FL. There are 16 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are private. Amenities include lights.

name: Stonecrest Community
description: Stonecrest is a gated, private community.  Guests are welcome by invitation only.  The courts are not open to the public without an invitation.  Guest must be registered with Security personnel and the system prior to arriving.  A driver's license is required for entry into the community.
